title: Neural probe for electrostimulation or recording and fabrication process for such a probe
abstract: For improving the electroactivity and long-term stability of a neural interface, a novel neural probe (1) is proposed that is formed from a fiber (6), preferably by thermal imprinting, and wherein a polymer thin film (5) is employed for carrying a conducting thin film to be used as a recording or stimulation electrode (4). Due to this specific choice of materials and design, the electrode (4) is rendered compliant with respect to the fiber (6) on a nanometer to micrometer scale and offers a surface that is tailor-made for adhering to nervous tissue.
claims:
Claims Patent claims 1. Neural probe (1) comprising a carrier body (2) forming a contact area (3), in particular for contacting brain tissue, and at least one electrode (4) arranged within the contact area (3), characterized in that a soft thin film (5) carries the at least one electrode (4), wherein the soft thin film (5) is carried by a carrier body (2), in particular wherein the soft thin film (5) is softer than the carrier body (2) . 2. Neural probe (1) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the at least one electrode (4) is an electrical stimulation and/or an electrical recording electrode and/or wherein the carrier body (3) is a fiber (6), preferably with an outer diameter of less than 0.8 mm and/or more than 0.05 mm, most preferably of less than 0.5 mm and more than 0.05 mm and/or wherein the soft thin film (5) is elastic and/or has a Young ' s modulus at least 10 3 times, preferably at least 10 4 times, smaller than a Young ' s modulus of the carrier body (2) and or has a Young ' s modulus of less than 5 MPa, in particular less than 2 MPa or less than 1 MPa and/or wherein the soft thin film (5) has a thickness of less than 5 pm. 3. Neural probe (1) as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the at least one electrode (4) is formed as a thin film electrode (4), preferably of a metal or metal alloy and/or with a thickness of less than 50 nm, preferably less than 20 nm, and/or wherein the at least one electrode (4) forms and/or follows a micro- and/or nanoscale corrugation (7). 4. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding 

 claims, wherein the corrugation (7) is a corrugation (7) of an outer surface of the electrode (4) to be brought into contact with brain tissue and/or wherein the corrugation (7) shows nanoscale ripples (8), preferably wherein the corrugation (7) and/or the nanoscale ripples (8) run along a major direction, which forms an angle to a longitudinal axis (10) of the fiber (6), and/or wherein the soft thin film (5) itself shows a nanoscale corrugation (7) which is covered by the at least one electrode (4), preferably wherein the at least one electrode (4) is a thin film electrode of homogenous thickness, most preferably wherein the thin film electrode (4) repeats the corrugation (7) of the soft thin film (5) on its outer surface and/or the thin film electrode (4) covers sidewalls (9) of the, preferably nanoscale, corrugation (7) of the soft thin film (5) . 5. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the contact area (3) forms a microscale structure (11), for example in the form of a microscale protrusion or a microscale recess (22), preferably wherein the at least one electrode (4) and/or the microscale structure (11) is covered by the soft thin film (5) and/or wherein the microscale structure (11) features a dimension in a first direction (16) that is larger than a dimension in a second direction (17), preferably wherein the dimension in the second dimension (17) is less than 100 pm, in particular wherein the nanoscale ripples (8) run approximately parallel to the second direction (17) . 6. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the contact area (3) is formed by thermal and/or UV-assisted imprinting and/or from the 

 fiber material, preferably such that a surface area of the fiber (6) is locally increased at a location of the contact area (3) and/or wherein two contact areas (3,3') are formed on opposing side areas of the fiber (6), each of the two contact areas featuring at least one electrode (4, 4') 7. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fiber (6) is a polymer fiber and/or wherein the fiber (6) has a core (12) which is electrically conducting and/or wherein the fiber (6) features an electrical wiring (18) and/or has a core (12), in particular in the form of a stiff polymer or metal wire(s) (15), which is stiffer than a surrounding cladding (13) of the fiber (6), in particular wherein the core (12) is extractably arranged within the cladding (13) and/or wherein the polymer of the fiber (6) is an organic polymer, preferably polyurethane (PU) or polyethylene terephthalate (PET) . 8. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the Young's Modulus of the polymer of the soft thin film (5) is at least a factor of 10 3 lower than the Young's Modulus of the polymer of the fiber (6) and/or wherein the polymer of the soft thin film (5) is an elastomer, preferably a siloxane, preferably polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS). 9. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the soft thin film (5) has a thickness of less than 10 pm, preferably of less than 2 pm, and/or wherein the at least one electrode (4) has a thickness of less than 50 nm, preferably of less than 20 nm, most preferably of less than 10 nm and/or wherein the at 

 least one electrode (4) is linked to the soft thin film (5) by an intermediate adhesion promotion layer, in particular consisting of thiol-functionalized PDMS, preferably wherein the electrode is embedded into the adhesion promotion layer and/or wherein the at least one electrode (4) is patterned as a network of connected islands on the soft thin film (5), preferably wherein the electrode (4, 4') has a thickness above the percolation threshold, in particular below four times the percolation threshold. 10. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein an electrical wiring (18) is arranged, preferably printed and/or sputtered, on an outer surface of the fiber (6), in particular wherein the fiber (6) features electrical contacts pads on the outer surface connected to the electrical wiring (18) and/or an encapsulation (23) which insulates the wiring (18) and/or wherein the contact area (3) features an array of electrodes (4), preferably wherein each of the electrodes (4) of the array is arranged on a microscale mesa, most preferably such that neighboring electrodes (4) are separated by a microscale trench. 11. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the carrier body (2) carries a conductor (26), in particular wherein the conductor (26) is a conductive track or layer made of gold and/or platinum and/or titanium. 12. Neural probe (1) of claim 11, wherein the conductor (26) is produced by for physical vapor deposition, in particular by high-power impulse magnetron sputtering. 13. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the electrode (4) is formed by metal atoms or clusters (27), in particular gold and/or and/or platinum and/or titanium, embedded in the soft thin film (5), in particular wherein the electrode (4) is electrically supplied by a or the conductor (26) beneath the soft thin film (5) . 14. Neural probe (1) of claim 13, wherein the metal atoms or clusters (27) are covalently bound to thiol sites within the soft thin film (5) . 15. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the carrier body (2) and/or the contact area (3) is/are stretchable without tearing apart, in particular enabling a strain of at least 10%, preferably at least 20%, preferably at least 30%, preferably at least 40%, preferably at least 50%, preferably at least 60%, preferably at least 70%, preferably at least 80%, preferably at least 90%, preferably at least 100%, more preferably of more than 100%. 16. Neural probe (1) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the carrier body (2) has a micro- and/or nanoscale corrugation (7), which defines a or the corrugation (7) of the electrode (4) and/or the soft thin film (5), in particular wherein the corrugation (7) of the carrier body (2) is made by thermal imprinting and/or UV-assisted imprinting. 17. Neural probe (1) of claim 16, wherein the corrugation (7) of the carrier body (2) is covered with a soft thin film (5) having a constant or almost constant thickness or the carrier body (2) is covered 

 with a soft thin film (5) having a variable thickness, in particular wherein gauges (28) of the carrier body's (2) corrugation (7) are completely filled out by the soft thin film (5) . 18. Neural probe bundle (19) comprising at least two neural probes (2) as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, in particular wherein the bundle (19) offers multiple electrodes (4), each positioned at different axial and/or radial positions along the bundle (19) and/or wherein some probes (1) of the bundle (19) are configured for stimulating nervous tissue while the remaining probes (1) of the bundle (19) are configured for recording neural signals. 19. Process for fabricating a neural probe (1), in particular according to one of the claims 1 to 17, featuring a polymer thin film (5) which carries at least one electrode (4) of the probe (1), preferably wherein the carrier body (2) forms a contact area (3) within which the at least one electrode (4) is arranged, characterized in that the polymer thin film (5) is deposited from a gas or liquid phase and cross-linked during deposition (in-situ curing), preferably by exposure to UV-wavelengths or a plasma. 20. Process according to claim 19, wherein an electrode (4), in particular the at least one electrode (4), is deposited on a, preferably deformable, nanoscale corrugation (7), in particular wherein the nanoscale corrugation (7) is formed in the polymer thin film (5) and/or wherein the polymer thin film (5) is deposited by molecular beam deposition, by electro-spray-deposition, preferably wherein the polymer thin film (5) is cured 

 and/or annealed, in particular by an exposure to UV wavelengths, during its deposition. 21. Process according to claim 19 or 20, wherein, prior to or during deposition of the electrode (4) onto the polymer thin film (5), the polymer thin film (5) is treated by a plasma, preferably an oxygen-plasma, in particular such that the nanoscale surface corrugation (7), for example in the form of nanoscale wrinkles or nanoscale ripples (8), is formed in the soft thin film (5) prior or during deposition of the electrode (4) . 22. Process according to any one of the claims 19 to 21, wherein the at least one electrode (4) is deposited such that a network of connected and electrically conducting islands is formed on the soft thin film (5), in particular such that an areal portion of the conducting network is near, preferably above, the percolation threshold and/or wherein the at least one electrode (4) is deposited, preferably on a nanoscale surface corrugation (8) of the polymer thin film (5), such that a compressive stress in the at least one electrode (4) is smaller than a critical stress of a heterostructure consisting of the at least one electrode (4) and underlying polymer thin film (5) . 23. Process as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein before the formation of the polymer thin film (5) a micro- and/or nanoscale corrugation (7) at the contact area (3) of the carrier body (2) is formed. 24. Process as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein before the formation of the polymer thin film (5) and/or after the formation of a micro- and/or 

 nanoscale corrugation (7) at the contact area (3) of the carrier body (2), a conductor (26) is formed on the carrier body (2), in particular for electrically contacting the electrode (4), preferably wherein the conductor (26) is formed as a conductive track, which is applied on the carrier body (2) by sputter coating. 25. Process as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the electrode (4) is formed by embedding metal atoms or clusters within the polymer thin film (5), in particular wherein the used metal atoms or clusters are gold and/or platinum and/or titanium, preferably wherein the metal atoms or clusters form covalent bonds to thiol sites of the polymer thin film (5) .
